conditions: Effect depends on the proportion of malic acid present at the start of MLF. High-malic wines (cool climate, young vintage) see a greater reduction. Wines with little malic acid (warm climate, advanced ripeness) see a minimal effect. Tartaric acid component is completely unaffected.
explanation: MLF converts malic acid (diprotic: 2 H⁺ per molecule) to lactic acid (monoprotic: 1 H⁺ per molecule) plus CO₂. The conversion reduces total titratable acidity and raises pH slightly (typically 0.1–0.2 pH units). The reduction is partial — tartaric acid, the dominant acid in most wines, is unaffected by MLF. The sensory outcome is softer, rounder perceived acidity.
